name: Big Oatmeal Cookies with Dates and Nuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover a flat baking sheet with parchment paper if possible; use tinfoil and butter it generously if necessary.
2. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
3. Cream together the butter and brown sugar until light and fluffy. The sugars should be well mixed in, but not completely dissolved.
4. Beat the eggs with milk and almond extract until foamy.
5. Sift flour, cinnamon, allspice, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into a bowl.
6. Prepare all embellishments (dates, walnuts, coconut, sesame seeds) separately.
7. Using a wooden spoon, incorporate about half of the flour mixture into the butter and sugar until well combined.
8. Add the egg mixture to the butter mixture and combine thoroughly.
9. Add the remaining flour mixture, then fold in oats, dates, walnuts, coconut, and sesame seeds. Mix only enough to ensure even distribution of ingredients.
10. Form golf ball-sized dollops of dough on the prepared baking sheet, spacing them about 8 inches apart.
11. Flatten each dollop into a circle that is approximately 3.5 inches in diameter (will spread slightly in the oven).
12. Ensure the cookies are not more than 1/4 inch thick.
13. Bake for 8 minutes, then rotate the baking sheet and lower the temperature to 350°F (175°C). Continue baking for an additional 5 to 8 minutes, or until the cookies are nicely browned and dry in the center when broken.
14. Allow cookies to cool on a wire rack.
Source: Yankee New England Cookbook by Leslie Land
